name: Saint Patrick's Day Irish Soda Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the crusty bread in wedges to serve. Add two teaspoons of grated lemon peel to complement the flavor of the currants.
2. In a large bowl, combine the first six ingredients.
3. Cut in the butter with a pastry blender or work it in with your fingers until combined with the dry ingredients.
4. Mix the egg and buttermilk together and then add this mixture to the dry ingredients. Stir until well blended.
5. Add the currants and stir the mixture well.
6. Turn out on a floured surface and knead gently for 3 minutes or until the dough is smooth.
7. Divide the dough into two pieces, shaping each into a round loaf.
8. Place each in a greased 8-inch cake or pie pan, pressing it down until the dough fills the pan.
9. Use a sharp knife to cut a half-inch deep cross on top of each loaf.
10. Bake in a preheated 375°F oven for about 40 minutes or until the bread sounds hollow when thumped.
11. Turn out on a wire rack to cool. Do not cut for about 4 hours. Makes 2 loaves.
